title: A solid circle with a square void at its center. The circle is the complete, closed Precept contract — the governed domain with no outside. The square port is the Inspect operation made physical: always open, always exact, impossible to close because it is a structural absence in the material. Precept guarantees that at any state, at any time, you can look inside and see every possible outcome without executing anything. The port is not a feature — it is the architecture.
conceptualBridge: The circle-square port means Precept is both a closed domain and a permanently transparent one because the circular boundary makes the contract total while the square void is a structurally built-in inspection window that cannot be sealed.
decision: Kept: direct and specific embodiment of the inspect operation; the circle-square combination is new to the set and passes the threshold, though the blue palette is overrepresented — consider a future iteration with a distinct color.
